title: Kind of Blue
label: Columbia
description: Every significant pressing of Miles Davis's Kind of Blue — the speed error, tape provenance, and a full reissue hierarchy from original Six Eye to AP UHQR.

title: A Love Supreme
label: Impulse!
description: Pressing guide for A Love Supreme — the Abbey Road safety tape, mono vs stereo, and every reissue from original orange Impulse! to the AP UHQR.

title: Time Out
label: Columbia
description: Collector-grade vinyl pressing guide for The Dave Brubeck Quartet's Time Out — Columbia Six Eye identification, Classic Records and Analogue Productions reissue hierarchy, and buyer's guide.

title: The Shape of Jazz to Come
label: Atlantic
description: Collector-grade vinyl pressing guide for Ornette Coleman's The Shape of Jazz to Come — Atlantic bullseye label identification, ORG, Speakers Corner and VMP reissue hierarchy, and buyer's guide.

title: Giant Steps
label: Atlantic
description: Collector-grade vinyl pressing guide for John Coltrane's Giant Steps — Atlantic black label identification, mono vs. stereo analysis, AP Atlantic 75 and Rhino 60th Anniversary reissue hierarchy, and buyer's guide.

title: Mingus Ah Um
label: Columbia
description: Pressing guide for Mingus Ah Um — the edit problem, Six Eye identification, and every pressing from original Columbia to the ORG 45rpm and MoFi One-Step.

title: Bitches Brew
label: Columbia
description: Collector-grade vinyl pressing guide for Miles Davis's Bitches Brew — Columbia 360 Sound two-eye label identification, Mobile Fidelity and One-Step reissue hierarchy, tape provenance, and buyer's guide.

title: Getz/Gilberto
label: Verve
description: Pressing guide for Getz/Gilberto — the channel reversal problem, Phil Ramone's original master tape, and every pressing from 1964 Verve to the Impex 1Step.

title: Moanin'
artist: Art Blakey and the Jazz Messengers
label: Blue Note
description: Pressing guide for Moanin' — Blue Note original identification, the A vs A-1 matrix debate, and every pressing from original Van Gelder to Music Matters SRX.

title: Saxophone Colossus
label: Prestige
description: Collector-grade vinyl pressing guide for Sonny Rollins's Saxophone Colossus — Prestige yellow label identification, RVG stamp authentication, Analogue Productions 200g and European pressing hierarchy, and buyer's guide.

title: Head Hunters
label: Columbia
description: Collector-grade vinyl pressing guide for Herbie Hancock's Head Hunters — Columbia KC 32731 original pressing identification, Analogue Productions 45rpm and 33rpm reissue hierarchy, and buyer's guide.

title: Speak No Evil
label: Blue Note
description: Pressing guide for Speak No Evil — Van Gelder's Englewood Cliffs peak, native mono and stereo tapes, and every reissue from original New York USA label to Music Matters AAA.

title: Out to Lunch!
label: Blue Note
description: Pressing guide for Out to Lunch! — Dolphy's final album as leader, mono strongly preferred, and every reissue from original Blue Note to Tone Poet.

title: The Köln Concert
label: ECM
description: Pressing guide for The Köln Concert — the near-cancelled solo piano session, German label variants, and the 2010 Pallas 180g as the modern reference.

title: Somethin' Else
label: Blue Note
description: Pressing guide for Somethin' Else — the final Hackensack session with Miles Davis on loan, mono only, and every reissue from original Blue Note 47 West 63rd to the MoFi One-Step.

title: Song for My Father
label: Blue Note
description: Pressing guide for Song for My Father — three Van Gelder sessions, two quintets, the bossa nova inspiration, and the Blue Note Classic Vinyl as the only widely available modern reissue.

title: Maiden Voyage
label: Blue Note
description: Pressing guide for Maiden Voyage — native mono and stereo tapes, Tony Williams's drums in stereo, and every reissue from original New York USA label to Music Matters AAA.

title: The Black Saint and the Sinner Lady
label: Impulse!
description: Pressing guide for The Black Saint and the Sinner Lady — first jazz album to use overdubbing, Bob Simpson engineering, and every reissue from original orange Impulse! to the AP 45rpm and Acoustic Sounds series.

title: The Sidewinder
label: Blue Note
description: Pressing guide for The Sidewinder — Blue Note's best-selling record ever, the boogaloo groove, and every reissue from original New York USA label to Music Matters 45rpm.

title: Blue Train
label: Blue Note
description: Pressing guide for Blue Train — Coltrane's only Blue Note leadership session, Hackensack living room acoustics, the NY23 label chronology, and every reissue from original to Tone Poet mono.

title: Birth of the Cool
label: Capitol
description: Pressing guide for Birth of the Cool — three Capitol sessions 1949–50, mono only (no stereo was ever made), turquoise label variants, and the Tone Poet 2026 as the definitive modern pressing.

title: Sunday at the Village Vanguard
label: Riverside
